macro_segments.title: The Status of a Torah Scroll with 85 Scattered Letters and the Vayehi Binsoa Ha'aron Passage

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Sefer Torah with 85 scattered letters retains sacred status
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's position: the Vayehi Binsoa passage will eventually be relocated
The two punishments separated by Vayehi Binsoa: initial murmuring and later complaints
Rabbi Hama bar Hanina: they turned away from God upon leaving Mount Sinai
Rashi and Tosafot explain the process of complaint beginning immediately after Sinai
Where the passage belongs: according to Rav Ashi, among the description of camps

macro_segments.title: Sacred Status of Margins and Blank Parchment in a Torah Scroll

micro_segments.title: Opening question: Do margins of a Torah scroll have sacred status requiring rescue from fire?
Worn-out vs. erased Torah scrolls: parchment dependence on presence of writing
The Gemara's challenge: intentional blank spaces should have their own status
Margins cause impurity (tumah) when part of intact Torah scroll
Heretical Torah scrolls and margins: the Gemara's wordplay on gilyonim and evangelion

macro_segments.title: Books of Heretics and the Status of Heretical Writings with Divine Names

micro_segments.title: Burning books of heretics that contain God's name: competing positions of Rabbi Yose and Rabbi Tarfon
Rabbi Tarfon's extreme stance: burn even names of God in heretical texts due to existential threat
Rabbi Yishmael's kal vachomer: peace between spouses justifies erasing God's name, so removing heretical threat does more
Not rescuing heretical texts from any form of destruction as collective policy

macro_segments.title: Books of Bei Avidan and Participation in Non-Jewish Intellectual Gatherings

micro_segments.title: Question about Bei Avidan: unclear status of texts from a mixed intellectual library
Definition of Bei Avidan: either a philosophical disputation center or a comprehensive library of all nations
Rav's refusal and Shmuel's cautious approach to Bei Avidan and Christian gatherings
The story of Ima Shalom and the philosopher: mocking Christian supersessionism through legal debate

macro_segments.title: Reading from Sacred Writings on Shabbat: the Ketuvim Prohibition

micro_segments.title: Why not read from Ketuvim on Shabbat: preventing people from abandoning the study hall
Rav's position: prohibition applies only during beit midrash hours; Shmuel extends it always
Evidence from Nehardea: reading Ketuvim at mincha time on Shabbat despite Shmuel's view
Rabbi Nehemia's rationale: preventing reading of secular documents through a stringency on Ketuvim
Secular documents on Shabbat and weekday: defining the parameters of prohibited reading

topical_tags.term: Sefer Torah bifnei atzmo (Torah scroll as independent unit)
Shem Hashem (Divine name in heretical texts)
Rabbi Tarfon (strict opposition to heretical texts)
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel (position on Vayehi Binsoa)
Rashi (interpretation of margins and writing dependency)
Tosafot (discussion of heretical writings and letters)
Ima Shalom (wife of Rabbi Eliezer, sister of Rabban Gamliel)
